methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Galvanick is a Defense & Security company at the SPAC stage founded by Joshua Steinman, Brandon Park, and Feliks Pleszczynski, having raised $20M+. The relevant comp universe encompasses 21 same-sector peers, including 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms. Notable private peers include Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. While 21 Defense & Security companies exist in the broader peer set, no sufficient subset meets the filtering criteria for reliable comparison—specifically, zero same-stage peers, zero adjacent-stage peers, and zero similar-tier peers were identified. The narrow filters require a minimum of three datapoints; relying on an all-sector median would lack precision and would not reflect stage-specific or capital-intensity dynamics material to Galvanick's positioning.

Without triangulation via peer multiples, alternative methods (discounted cash flow, precedent transactions, or asset-based approaches) would be required to establish a defensible valuation range. The primary constraint is the absence of sufficient comparables at Galvanick's stage and capital tier within its sector. This limitation underscores the difficulty in valuing early-stage frontier-tech companies in concentrated sectors, where stage heterogeneity and capital requirements diverge significantly across the peer set. Further data on recent rounds in adjacent Defense & Security subsectors may help narrow the range.
